title: Authors and Citation

code: @Manual{,   title = {ripsabr: Access RIPSA Health Indicators},   author = {Raphael Saldanha},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://rfsaldanha.github.io/ripsabr/}, }

title: Access RIPSA Health Indicators
text: Pacote R para recuperacao de indicadores da RIPSA como tibbles padronizadas. Fontes principais: Fichas de Qualificacao dos Indicadores: https://www.ripsa.org.br/fichasidb Dados abertos SUS com bases RIPSA: https://dadosabertos.saude.gov.br/dataset?q=ripsa Os dados sao baixados diretamente dos recursos CKAN Portal de Dados Abertos SUS.

title: Access RIPSA Health Indicators
text: list_indicators() inclui cobertura disponível em cada arquivo de origem: spatial_granularity time_granularity time_start, time_end available_categories, available_category_names get_indicator() retorna uma tibble com colunas consistentes para qualquer nivel geografico: indicator_code, indicator_name, theme, dimension period, date geo_level, geo_code, geo_name value, unit category_code, category update_date Use list_categories() para consultar categorias disponíveis e filtre com category_id, category_code ou category_name: O padrao category = NULL mantem todas categorias existentes arquivo de origem.
code: library(ripsabr)  list_indicators()  list_categories("MRB.1.01")  sarampo_am <- get_indicator(   code = "MRB.1.01",   geo = "state",   geo_code = 13,   category = "total" ) list_categories("COB.4.01")  prenatal <- get_indicator(   code = "COB.4.01",   geo_code = 130260,   category = "nascidos_vivos_com_7_ou_mais_consultas" )

title: Download a RIPSA indicator — get_indicator
text: get_indicator() downloads indicator identified code returns standardized time series requested geographic level.

title: Download a RIPSA indicator — get_indicator
code: get_indicator(   code,   geo = c("municipality", "state", "region", "health_region", "macro_region", "country"),   geo_code = NULL,   category = NULL,   category_id = NULL )

title: Download a RIPSA indicator — get_indicator
text: code Character scalar. RIPSA indicator code, example "MRB.1.01". geo Character scalar. Geographic level return. One "municipality", "state", "region", "health_region", "macro_region", "country". geo_code Optional vector geographic codes keep. Codes compared character values. category Optional vector category codes names keep. Use "total" total/-categories rows available source file. can also pass category name returned list_categories(), "Nascidos vivos com 7 ou mais consultas". Defaults NULL, keeping every category present source file. category_id Optional vector category ids returned list_categories().

title: Download a RIPSA indicator — get_indicator
text: tibble standardized columns: indicator_code, indicator_name, theme, dimension, period, date, geo_level, geo_code, geo_name, value, unit, category_code, category, update_date. legacy RIPSA files expose one numerator, category_code category identify numerator used calculate value.

title: Download a RIPSA indicator — get_indicator
text: easiest way choose categories call list_categories(code) use returned category_id, category_code, category_name. files RIPSA category columns, category can receive full category_code returned function, "TC:0", category prefix, "TC". Passing prefix keeps every item category. category prefixes currently found RIPSA CKAN indicator files : "API", "COR", "cor_raca", "EA", "ESP", "ETI", "EV", "FE", "FER", "fx_etaria", "fx_rdpc", "FXETC1", "FXETC2", "GC", "GIF", "Idade", "IG", "PESSOA1", "sap", "SD", "sexo", "SFE", "SG", "sitdom", "SVA", "TC", "TD", "tipoarea", "TPC". legacy numerator/denominator files, source expose fixed category vocabulary. files, category_code generated make.names() numerator column label removing "Numerador - " prefix. Use category = NULL inspect available category_code values specific indicator.

title: RIPSA indicator category metadata — indicator_categories
text: dataset standardized category metadata RIPSA indicator.

title: RIPSA indicator category metadata — indicator_categories
text: tibble columns: indicator_code RIPSA indicator code. category_id Sequential category id within indicator. category_code Standardized category code use get_indicator(). category_name Standardized category label. category_source_code Raw source category code available. category_source_name Raw source category label available.

title: RIPSA indicator metadata — indicators
text: dataset RIPSA indicators currently registered package.

title: RIPSA indicator metadata — indicators
text: tibble columns: id RIPSA indicator code. theme Indicator theme. dimension Indicator dimension. name Indicator name. ckan_package CKAN package slug. ckan_resource CKAN resource id. ckan_resource_url CKAN resource page URL. download_url Current CSV zip download URL exposed CKAN resource. spatial_granularity Comma-separated geographic levels available source file. time_granularity Temporal granularity inferred source file. time_start First date available source file. time_end Last date available source file. available_categories Comma-separated standardized category codes available source file. available_category_names Pipe-separated standardized category labels available source file.

what: List categories available for a RIPSA indicator — list_categories
title: List categories available for a RIPSA indicator — list_categories
List categories available for a RIPSA indicator — list_categories
List categories available for a RIPSA indicator — list_categories
text: code Character scalar. RIPSA indicator code, example "COB.4.01".

title: List categories available for a RIPSA indicator — list_categories
text: tibble one row per category available indicator source file. category_code category_name standardized package users. category_source_code category_source_name contain raw source values available. Use category_id, category_code, category_name get_indicator().

title: List available RIPSA indicators — list_indicators
text: tibble one row per indicator currently registered package metadata.
